reasoning: Paracoccidioidomycosis is a fungal infection with no existing vaccine. A 2023 review confirms all PCM vaccine candidates are in preclinical stages. There are no approved human fungal vaccines in existence. Given the timeline of vaccine development—requiring several phases of human trials—it is functionally impossible for a vaccine to be developed, approved, and appropriately listed on Wikipedia by December 2025. The marginal increase in probability for more distant dates merely accounts for the small nonzero chance of persistent Wikipedia vandalism or erroneous editing on the respective resolution dates.
